heading: What We Expect from Every Player
text: Good RP isn't just about knowing the rules — it's about adding to the world, not subtracting from it. Every interaction should leave things more interesting than you found them.

heading: 1. Fear RP
text: Your character values their life. When there's a gun in your face, act like it. You cannot ignore threats to your life just because you think you could 'win' the situation OOC. Running from someone actively pointing a firearm at you (within 10 feet)

heading: 2. New Life Rule (NLR)
text: After your character is downed and revived by EMS (or respawns), they have fuzzy memories of the events leading to their death. You cannot return to active combat zones for 15 minutes after being revived. You cannot use OOC knowledge from before your

heading: 3. Powergaming
text: Powergaming = forcing unrealistic outcomes or using mechanics in ways that deny others meaningful roleplay. Don't use /me to make other characters do things (e.g., '/me grabs John's gun' — you don't get to decide what happens to John's gun). Don't ex

heading: 4. Metagaming
text: Information from Discord, Twitch streams, or OOC chats cannot be used in-character. Radio channels are IC. Don't create private Discord calls to relay IC information . This includes weekly meetings for organizations. Churches should be held IC only a

heading: 5. Random Deathmatch (RDM) & Vehicle Deathmatch (VDM)
text: RDM: Don't attack or kill someone without a valid RP reason. Boredom is not a valid reason. VDM: Don't use your vehicle as a weapon . There must be prior RP engagement before violence. Drive-bys on strangers with zero prior interaction = instant puni

heading: 6. Combat
text: Don't disconnect to avoid being robbed, arrested, killed, or facing any other IC consequence. If your game crashes mid-scene, return ASAP and notify the other parties. Utilize the headpops channels. In Police vs Criminal combat: Police are allowed up

heading: 7. RP Continuity & Scene Endings
text: Don't break character in the middle of active scenes without a genuine emergency. If you need to pause a scene, try your absolute best to RP a reason for it, ie your character needs to use the bathroom, take a phone call. Make use of emotes if you ha

heading: 8. Sensitive RP Topics
text: Last Call RP allows darker RP themes as part of storytelling — but with guardrails. ERP is not allowed in the city, we do not want 50 shades of gray in the server logs. Sexual RP involving minors is an immediate permanent ban. N O EXCEPTIONS!!! Tortu

heading: Why Vehicle Rules Matter
text: Cars are one of the most common sources of RP issues on any server.

heading: 1. Drive Realistically
text: Drive at speeds that make sense for the road and situation. Nobody in real life is doing 200mph through downtown. We have spee d limits. Consult the penal code for specifics. Or ask a member of PD. Obey traffic laws when there's no active RP reason n

heading: 2. Vehicle Deathmatch (VDM)
text: Do NOT use your vehicle as a weapon . Hit-and-runs on players require active, ongoing RP justification. Ramming police vehicles during a chase must have a proportionate in-character reason. Pinning someone on foot with a vehicle to rob/detain them is

heading: 3. Vehicle Pursuits
text: Pursuit rules for LEOs are defined in the LEO SOP. Civilians: use good judgment and don't intentionally bait chases. Helicopter griefing (hovering over a scene just to be annoying) is a violation.

heading: 4. Vehicle hiding in pursuit
text: If you escape the police, do not immediately park your vehicle in a garage. Give them a few minutes before parking it. Your car is an extension of your character — treat it like one. A wheelman has a rep. Don't blow it on a random. Last Call RP — Veh

heading: Meet the Staff Team
text: Staff at Last Call RP are volunteers who keep the lights on and the peace between scenes. They're players too — they have characters, crews, and stories. The difference is they're also responsible for making sure everyone gets a fair shot at good RP.

heading: 1. Staff Roles
text: Director : Final say on all policy, direction, and appeals . Lead : Oversees their departments. Developer: They develop features and bug fix. Staff : Handle a variety of things to keep things running smoothly. Moderator: Are the first in line to look

heading: 2. Tickets
text: If you have an issue, open a ticket, and a moderator will address it and determine where to escalate if needed.

heading: 3. Reporting a Player
text: Use the ticket system remember to i nclude: your name, their name, what happened, when it happened, and evidence if available. Don't report the same person multiple times for the same incident. One report, let staff handle it. Don't file r etaliatory

heading: 4. Appealing Decisions
text: Bans, and warnings can all be appealed through the Discord ticket system. Be specific and calm in your appeal. 'This is unfair' is not an argument. Explain what happened from your perspective. A moderator will review the appeals , and will escalate t

heading: 5. Staff Conduct Standards
text: Staff are held to the same rules as everyone else — plus a bit more. Staff cannot abuse their position for IC advantages (spawning items, teleporting unfairly, looking at player locations). Staff are not immune from bans. Abuse of power is taken seri

heading: 6. Contacting Staff
text: Use the ticketing system.

heading: 7. Staff Availability
text: Staff are volunteers. Response times vary. Peak hours see faster responses — off-peak may take longer. If something is urgent and no staff are available, document it (screenshot/video) and please be patient. Staff aren't here to police the fun — they
